title: grindelia latifolia subsp. platyphylla
intro: Grindelia latifolia subsp. platyphylla, also known as broad-leaved gumweed, is a plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits also include its ability to soothe and calm the body, reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Grindelia latifolia subsp. platyphylla can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible natural remedy.
